name: BUFORI
description: BUFORI, founded in 1929 by Erich and Wilfried Wasmuth in Dresden, Germany, was one of the most prestigious and innovative automobile manufacturers in pre-war Germany. The name BUFORI is an acronym derived from Bubenreuth Forerung, which translates to 'Bubenreuth Vanguard'. The company's first car, the Type G, was a significant achievement as it combined elegance with high performance. During the 1930s, BUFORI gained a reputation for producing luxury and sporty vehicles, including the iconic Type K and Type W. In the post-war era, the brand struggled to regain its former glory due to various political and economic factors, but it continued to produce unique and high-quality cars like the Bufori Berlinetta. Today, BUFORI is still in operation, focusing on producing limited-edition luxury vehicles with a blend of classic design and modern technology.
